comments.body:
### Edited/Blocked Notification

Renovate will not automatically rebase this PR, because it does not recognize the last commit author and assumes somebody else may have edited the PR.

You can manually request rebase by checking the rebase/retry box above.

 ⚠️ **Warning**: custom changes will be lost.

@nalind looking at the logs when you say getting killed you mean
```
[+1024s] # STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
[+1024s] # STEP 2/3: RUN apk add openssh-client
[+1024s] # fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
[+1024s] # fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
[+1024s] # container exited on killed
[+1024s] # Error: building at STEP "RUN apk add openssh-client": while running runtime: exit status 1
[+1024s] # [ rc=124 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
[+1024s] # *** TIMED OUT ***
```
?

Because that is a command timeout and thus it gets killed.

apk mirrors are known to be quite problematic for a while now, I had to remove it from podman,
https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/15ae942a48ca0fa9680d70438df472d2971e0680

In general I would strongly recommend against doing such runtime installs in CI tests due the normal network issues that brings in. If we need an image with ssh in it I suggest we either put that into our main testimage https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/system/build-testimage and push a new version to our quay.io repo and use these images here then or create another custom image based on that that we push.
Yeah, that was the one - apk kills itself, ugh.  I'll remove that commit, then.

it doesn't kill itself, we run all commands with timeout to avoid CI hangs for unnecessary time and then timeout sends the kill signal.

In theory you could bump the timeout but I have seen no evidence that more timeout actually helps so I rather not since it is already at 5min here

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/e07c86d5be38f42e3bfb4da3f00f2e0aaebceea8/tests/helpers.bash#L19
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/e07c86d5be38f42e3bfb4da3f00f2e0aaebceea8/tests/helpers.bash#L396

```
[+0746s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah run testctr -- sh -c git -C /podman-branch rev-parse HEAD
[+0746s] # 143ae3cd728608b34c198dcdeb56eaf66aea6875
[+0746s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah run testctr -- sh -c git -C /podman-branch ls-remote origin v5.0 | cut -f1
[+0746s] # fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/containers/podman.git/': OpenSSL SSL_read: OpenSSL/3.5.4: error:0A000126:SSL routines::unexpected eof while reading, errno 0
[+0746s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
[+0746s] # #|     FAIL: buildah run testctr -- sh -c git -C /podman-branch ls-remote origin v5.0 | cut -f1
[+0746s] # #| expected: = '143ae3cd728608b34c198dcdeb56eaf66aea6875'
[+0746s] # #|   actual:   'fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/containers/podman.git/': OpenSSL SSL_read: OpenSSL/3.5.4: error:0A000126:SSL routines::unexpected eof while reading, errno 0'
```

That is yet another network flake.

I cannot stress enough about things like https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6163 or https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6492. We really should not connect to any services from the internet within these tests IMO.

> ```
> [+0746s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah run testctr -- sh -c git -C /podman-branch rev-parse HEAD
> [+0746s] # 143ae3cd728608b34c198dcdeb56eaf66aea6875
> [+0746s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah run testctr -- sh -c git -C /podman-branch ls-remote origin v5.0 | cut -f1
> [+0746s] # fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/containers/podman.git/': OpenSSL SSL_read: OpenSSL/3.5.4: error:0A000126:SSL routines::unexpected eof while reading, errno 0
> [+0746s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
> [+0746s] # #|     FAIL: buildah run testctr -- sh -c git -C /podman-branch ls-remote origin v5.0 | cut -f1
> [+0746s] # #| expected: = '143ae3cd728608b34c198dcdeb56eaf66aea6875'
> [+0746s] # #|   actual:   'fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/containers/podman.git/': OpenSSL SSL_read: OpenSSL/3.5.4: error:0A000126:SSL routines::unexpected eof while reading, errno 0'
> ```
> 
> That is yet another network flake.

This does not appear to be a flake.  In the test VM, `podman run registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:43 curl -v -v https://www.redhat.com/` produces the same error message, while no error is produced either when retrieving the same location using plain http, or when running with `--network=host`.

I'm not an expert on how we set up networking, but as its default, pasta is setting the MTU for the container's default network interface to 65520.  Running `ip link ` in the container to lower it, and then running `curl` (or `git ls-remote`, as the failing test does) succeeds.

I can add a `--network=host` to the command that's failing, and it'll start passing, but I'm not going to be thrilled about it.
Okay, that appears to be specific to passt_0.0~git20251210.d04c480-1_amd64, fixed by updating to passt_0.0~git20251223.2ba9fd5-1_amd64.
